data.title: Genicom showcasing high-temp UV sensors at AFPE 2026 in Shanghai, with stable measurement up to 250°C using GaN tech.
data.description: Genicom Co., Ltd., headquartered in Seoul, South Korea, announced on February 18, 2026, the development of GaN-based technology enabling stable UV measurement in extreme environments up to 250°C. This move is aimed at enhancing the company's industrial UV measurement portfolio to cater to the global smart factory markets. The company, led by CEO Jung-hwan Son, is making strides in this field to meet the growing demand for such technologies. This development signifies a significant advancement, providing reliability and accuracy in UV measurements even under harsh conditions.

data.title: ZENVIA notified by Nasdaq for minimum bid price deficiency regarding its Class A common shares.
data.description: Today, Zenvia Inc. was informed by The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC about a notice regarding the closing bid price of the Company's Class A common shares. The notice stated that the Company’s shares no longer meet the listing qualifications set by Nasdaq. This development comes as of February 18, 2026, marking a significant event for the Company. The specific numbers and details of the bid price were not disclosed in the information provided.

data.title: Artemis Gold reports strong financials in Q4 2025 with $237M EBITDA; full year 2025 sees $630M EBITDA, $913.9M revenue, 192,808 ounces of gold produced, and plans for expansion.
data.description: Artemis Gold Inc. (TSXV: ARTG) reported its financial and operating results for Q4 2025 and full year 2025. Highlights include hitting lowest decile in AISC at US$869 per ounce, with EBITDA of $237 million in Q4 2025 and $630 million for the full year. Gold production reached 68,480 ounces in Q4, totaling 192,808 ounces for the year. Q4 sales of 67,852 ounces attracted an average realized price of US$4,168 per ounce. The company had Q4 revenue of $333.7 million and full year revenue of $913.9 million. Construction of Phase 1A expansion is on track, and EP2 expansion decision was announced. Cash and equivalents were $168.1 million at the end of 2025 with a total available liquidity of $410.1 million. Pro-forma available liquidity stood at $852.7 million post bond offering. CEO Dale Andres expressed confidence in the company's growth strategy. Operating results showed impressive figures from Q3 to Q4 2025, depicting the strong performance and growth trajectory of Artemis Gold Inc.

data.title: Revel Digital releases AI suite for digital signage with 6 capabilities for content creation, automation, and insights.
data.description: Revel Digital, a prominent cloud-based digital signage platform, has introduced six AI-powered capabilities to enhance natural language content creation, intelligent automation, and real-time network insights within the digital signage industry. This recent announcement marks the general availability of these features, showcasing the company's commitment to innovation.
